{"key_event":"1990 MMD formation and one-party repeal","reasoning":"Economic crisis, food riots, labor/church/student mobilization, and the emergence of the MMD made civil society a routine national constraint on UNIP rule. The state retained coercive and administrative reach, but debt, shortages, policy reversals, lost legitimacy, and signs of military disaffection reduced its effective capacity from the late-1970s peak.","society_change":1.7,"society_power":7.0,"state_change":-1.3,"state_power":6.7}
